name: Veritas Assurance
one_liner: Neutral, software-accelerated assurance body that audits/certifies AI systems against mandated regimes (EU AI Act high-risk, NYC LL144 hiring-bias, Colorado AI Act) and issues attestations for regulators/customers/insurers.
buyer: AI providers/deployers needing conformity + enterprises needing third-party vendor assurance.
wedge: Start with the audit already legally forced and not self-attestable: NYC LL144 employment-bias audits, then EU AI Act Annex III conformity as notified-body designation matures.
differentiation: Big 4 audits are manual/non-technical; lab governance suites are self-certification (non-credible). Independent + automated continuous re-audit at ~1/10 cost. Neutrality is the product.
bulldozer_distance: Vendors structurally barred from certifying themselves; regulators require independence -> labs/hyperscalers cannot take this revenue. Accreditation+reputation moat. 24-36mo.
pays_today_evidence: NYC LL144 mandates annual INDEPENDENT bias audits now; EU AI Act high-risk applies Aug 2 2026 with notified-body shortage; AI governance spend ~$492M 2026.
fast_cash_8wk: Yes: LL144 bias audits + EU AI Act readiness/gap assessments as billable engagements ($8-25k).
rough_25k_path: 3-5 LL144 audits or one EU readiness assessment at $8-25k in 8 weeks.

name: Halve
one_liner: Distills a team's own frontier-agent traffic into task-specific small models serving the majority of calls at 10-100x lower cost, quality-gated by replay vs logged frontier outputs.
buyer: Eng leaders / FinOps at companies hitting per-engineer AI caps (Uber pattern) and high-volume agent operators.
wedge: Mine the customer's agent logs, auto-distill task-scoped SLMs, route simple sub-tasks to them and hard reasoning to frontier, promote an SLM only after replay clears a quality bar.
differentiation: Routers (LiteLLM/OpenRouter) are commoditized; this MANUFACTURES cheaper per-customer models from their own traffic = 10x bill cut + a per-customer data moat.
bulldozer_distance: 18mo+. Labs structurally disincentivized (cannibalizes API revenue); per-customer distilled models on private traffic are data lock-in.
pays_today_evidence: 'Uber exhausted its 2026 AI budget in 4 months; caps $1,500/mo per employee.' NVIDIA arXiv:2506.02153: SLMs give 10-100x agentic cost reduction, unproductized by labs.
fast_cash_8wk: Yes: paid POC showing X% bill reduction; savings-share or flat fee.
rough_25k_path: 3-5 high-spend teams on savings-share or $5-8k/mo flat.

name: Declarant
one_liner: CargoWise-native customs agent that classifies each line to the correct HTS code, files ABI/ACE entries with a licensed broker in the loop, and claws back overpaid duties.
buyer: Mid-market customs brokers and high-volume importers (licensed broker / trade-compliance manager).
wedge: Open with retroactive duty-recovery audit (1520(d) refunds) on 12 months of entries, contingency-priced, then move upstream to live classification + entry filing.
differentiation: Distilled HTS classifier on importer's own entries + CBP CROSS rulings + broker-in-loop ABI filing inside CargoWise. No lab can file to CBP or own the CROSS+correction dataset.
bulldozer_distance: CBP Jan-2026 ruling requires licensed broker for filings (regulatory moat); CargoWise slow; per-importer dataset compounds. 18-24mo.
pays_today_evidence: Cervo $5M seed, Trava (YC), Nexcade $2.5M; 2026 tariff escalation + White House customs-enforcement action spiking willingness-to-pay.
fast_cash_8wk: Yes: no-cost duty-recovery audit, file refunds, take 25-30%; first $ on first refund check.

name: Recert
one_liner: Yardi/RealPage-native agent that completes LIHTC/HUD income certifications, files TRACS/50059, keeps every unit audit-ready.
buyer: Affordable-housing owner/operators, PHAs, compliance teams (compliance director / asset manager).
wedge: Automate annual income re-certs first (verification, rent/utility calc, tenant-file assembly), then TRACS submission + audit prep.
differentiation: Encodes HUD 4350.3 + state HFA + LIHTC logic into a checker driving Yardi Affordable. Generic LLMs hallucinate these rules and can't operate the system of record.
bulldozer_distance: Labs won't carry compliance liability or build Yardi RPA; market-rate proptech won't pivot into the niche; per-state rule+finding dataset compounds. 24-36mo.
pays_today_evidence: LIHTC/HUD compliance mandatory + brutally manual; operators pay third-party compliance consultants; market-rate leasing AI (EliseAI) proves multifamily pays for agents.
fast_cash_8wk: Yes-ish: paid pilot clearing a recert backlog before an audit; invoice per-unit/property.

name: Allowable
one_liner: Deltek Costpoint-native agent running DCAA-compliant timekeeping, contract-mod/CLIN tracking, and incurred-cost submissions for federal contractors.
buyer: Small/mid federal contractors (controller / contracts manager + DCAA/finance).
wedge: Automate annual incurred-cost submission + DCAA timekeeping monitoring first, then contract-mod ingestion + CLIN/funding tracking.
differentiation: RAG over FAR/DFARS/DCAM mapped onto live Costpoint job-cost+timesheet data for audit-ready submissions. Labs can't operate in Costpoint or assume DCAA-finding liability; pre-award startups wedged elsewhere.
bulldozer_distance: Deep FAR/DFARS/DCAA + GovCon-ERP lock-in; labs avoid liability+CUI; allowability+audit dataset compounds. 24-36mo.
pays_today_evidence: GovDash $40M+, Sweetspot/CLEATUS funded (all pre-award); DCAA audits mandatory; contractors pay consultants for incurred-cost (ICS/ICE) + CPSR.
fast_cash_8wk: Slow-ish: consulting-style pilot doable but procurement+security review push first $ to 8-12 weeks.

name: Mainspring
one_liner: Autonomous COBOL/mainframe-to-modern migration agent + a proprietary equivalence-verification harness proving the rewrite is behaviorally identical (audit-grade gate banks need to decommission the mainframe).
buyer: CIOs / modernization leads at banks, insurers, government on COBOL/JCL/CICS/VSAM.
wedge: Start with one batch subsystem: translate, auto-generate an equivalence harness from recorded mainframe I/O (record/replay parity), report line-by-line behavioral equivalence for audit/risk.
differentiation: General coding agents translate files but can't PROVE audit-grade equivalence or handle COBOL/JCL/CICS context; the verification harness is the moat.
bulldozer_distance: 24-36mo: domain+regulatory+deployment-data moat; labs won't earn COBOL+audit credibility fast; deep enterprise lock-in.
pays_today_evidence: a16z $3T AI-coding/legacy-replacement thesis; vertical AI spend tripled to $3.5B; regulated buyers pay for verification.
fast_cash_8wk: Slow: enterprise cycles; first $ = paid assessment/POC on one subsystem ($25-100k), closes in quarters.
rough_25k_path: One bank/insurer POC at $25-100k; quarters not weeks.

name: CompuQuote
one_liner: Vendr-for-compute: proprietary benchmark of what companies actually pay per GPU-hour/token across CoreWeave/Lambda/hyperscalers/Together/Baseten + an agent that renegotiates compute contracts.
buyer: CFOs / FinOps / heads of infra at AI-heavy startups + enterprises with $1M+ annual compute spend.
wedge: Free GPU/inference price-check ('is your CoreWeave quote above market?') ingesting invoices/quotes, building the only cross-buyer compute price dataset; upsell renewal + autonomous negotiation.
differentiation: Vendr/Tropic own SaaS; nobody owns compute. Real discounts live in private MSAs clouds won't publish. Once aggregated, the benchmark is unreplicable.
bulldozer_distance: Hyperscalers structurally conflicted (won't expose discounting); labs don't sell procurement; benchmark compounds per invoice. 18mo+.
pays_today_evidence: Hyperscaler AI capex $665B 2026; Uber blew budget in 4 months; ~60% CFOs plan 10%+ AI-finance spend increase; Vendr/Tropic prove buyers pay for price benchmarks.
fast_cash_8wk: FAST: contingency 15-20% of first-year savings; one $1M contract at 20% = ~$30-40k fee in 8 weeks via warm CFO intros.

name: Cleanroom
one_liner: Data clean-room sandbox where agents work against format-preserving tokenized shadows of production PII/PHI, re-identifying real values only on human-approved, logged actions.
buyer: Security / data-protection leads at regulated enterprises (healthcare, fintech, insurance) blocked from putting agents on real customer data.
wedge: One connector (Snowflake or support tickets): tokenize PII in-flight, hand agents the shadow, gate re-identification behind policy+approval. Land one regulated paid pilot.
differentiation: Data minimization (not a threat firewall) orthogonal to Lakera/Lasso. Agents never receive raw PII -> compromised agent has nothing to exfiltrate; 10x lower breach blast-radius. Format-preserving tokenization + JIT re-id with audit chain is a hard systems build.
bulldozer_distance: Labs ship guardrails/DLP signals, not a tokenization clean-room wired into data stores+approval workflows; integration+compliance evidence is the moat. 24-36mo.
pays_today_evidence: 55% report data-sovereignty issues; 43% had IP-leakage events; shadow AI in ~20% of breaches (+$670k each); hospitals/banks refuse public-cloud LLM APIs.
fast_cash_8wk: Moderate: paid pilot ($15-25k) with one regulated design partner; gate = their security review.
rough_25k_path: One regulated pilot at $25k or two at $12.5k.

name: Comptroller
one_liner: Spend-integrity layer on agent card rails (Lithic/Stripe Issuing/Ramp) tying every agent purchase to a cryptographic justification trace and blocking prompt-injection-driven spend that stays within card limits.
buyer: CFO/FinOps + platform teams giving autonomous agents real purchasing power (ad-buying, procurement, compute, SaaS).
wedge: Sit as the authorization webhook (Lithic ASA / Stripe Issuing auth), require a signed 'why' per transaction (task+policy+reasoning hash), flag spend not matching stated intent.
differentiation: Card platforms enforce limits but can't see agent reasoning, so they can't catch an injected agent buying from an attacker's in-budget merchant. Intent-fraud needs the task trace card networks don't hold.
bulldozer_distance: Card issuers won't ingest agent reasoning (privacy/liability); labs don't do payments; lives in the seam. Intent-fraud corpus compounds.
pays_today_evidence: Lithic/Stripe/Ramp shipped agent card issuance 2025-26; Uber capped agent spend after a 4-month blowout; agentic media-buying/procurement live.
fast_cash_8wk: Yes-ish: paid pilot ($8-15k) with 2-3 startups running agent media-buying/procurement.
rough_25k_path: 2-3 pilots at ~$10k then per-agent or %-of-spend pricing.

name: Caseworth
one_liner: Agent embedded in PointClickCare/MatrixCare that audits every MDS/PDPM assessment for under-coding then defends Medicare/Medicaid claims via ADR responses + denial appeals.
buyer: Regional skilled-nursing operators / SNF chains (MDS coordinator/DON + revenue-cycle director/CFO).
wedge: Land on pure-upside PDPM/Medicaid case-mix under-coding detection on last 90 days of assessments (contingency, no rip-and-replace), then ADR packet assembly + appeals.
differentiation: Forward-deployed agent reading/writing inside the EHR (no public API) with per-state RUG/PDPM logic + won-appeal precedent dataset. Horizontal chatbot can't operate the EHR or hold the appeal flywheel.
bulldozer_distance: Labs won't assume clinical/reimbursement liability or build SNF-EHR RPA; PointClickCare won't fight payers (adversarial to its payer relationships); cross-EHR + appeal data compounds. 24mo+.
pays_today_evidence: PointClickCare shipped Billing Advisor (June 2026) + MDS Advisor beta; Flax Health $3.5M, Teton.ai $20M; PDPM under-coding costs six figures/yr/facility.
fast_cash_8wk: Yes: free contingency audit of last-90-day assessments, bill 25-30% of recovered uplift.
rough_25k_path: One ~12-15 facility chain at ~$2k/facility/mo or contingency book.

name: Draw
one_liner: Agent generating AIA pay applications, certified (Davis-Bacon) payroll, and lien waivers from Procore/Sage, submitting through Textura/GCPay.
buyer: Specialty subcontractors and GCs on public/prevailing-wage work (controller / project accountant).
wedge: Start with certified payroll + prevailing-wage classification compliance (weekly, penalty-bearing, hated), then AIA G702/G703 pay-apps + lien waivers.
differentiation: Pulls timecards+cost codes from Sage/Foundation/Procore, applies per-jurisdiction prevailing-wage determinations, submits to Textura/GCPay across four no-API systems.
bulldozer_distance: Labs lack construction-ERP integration + won't assume false-certification liability; Procore won't drive payroll/wage compliance; per-jurisdiction wage+audit dataset compounds. 18-24mo.
pays_today_evidence: Certified-payroll services + lien-waiver processors already charge for this; Davis-Bacon false-cert penalties make it non-optional; construction AI funding concentrated in estimating, leaving back-office open.
fast_cash_8wk: Yes: paid pilot generating one sub's weekly certified payroll + first pay-app; invoice per-project/month.

name: ClaimSure
one_liner: Per-claim performance warranty on AI-generated medical codes: if a code we produce triggers a denial/clawback, we pay the shortfall.
buyer: Hospital revenue-cycle departments, physician-group billing companies, RCM BPOs.
wedge: Attach a warranty/guarantee to an existing AI-coding workflow for ONE specialty (outpatient E/M or emergency medicine) where denials are frequent and dollar-measurable; underwrite per-claim from own denial+appeal outcome data.
differentiation: Munich Re/Armilla underwrite at model level via one-time DD; ClaimSure prices risk per-claim from a proprietary denial/appeal flywheel no horizontal carrier or lab can see; payout tied to actual loss event.
bulldozer_distance: Labs disclaim liability in ToS + can't touch PHI-gated payer-outcome data; horizontal AI insurers lack per-specialty flywheel. Actuarial data compounds. 24-36mo.
pays_today_evidence: Providers pay coding vendors + lose billions to denials; Munich Re aiSure + Armilla ($25M limits 2026) prove enterprises buy AI performance guarantees; RCM is a named top-CFO budget line.
fast_cash_8wk: Slow on a bound policy (needs MGA/carrier paper), but sell a 90-day guaranteed-coding pilot/audit to one billing company for cash + seed loss data.
rough_25k_path: One mid billing company pays $25-40k for a 90-day single-specialty guaranteed-coding pilot.

name: Attestable
one_liner: Network + API putting a licensed human (physician, attorney, PE, CPA) in the loop to cryptographically sign off on AI outputs, wrapped in malpractice/E&O coverage, making AI output legally usable/billable in regulated workflows.
buyer: AI healthtech/legaltech/fintech vendors needing a licensed professional + liability chain to be compliant/reimbursable/court-admissible.
wedge: Start with one workflow legally requiring a credentialed signer (physician sign-off on AI clinical docs, or attorney attestation on AI filings) as a Twilio-style API.
differentiation: Labs aren't licensed professionals and can't assume professional liability; you own the credentialed network + insurance wrapper + audit trail; converts AI assistance into legally accountable billable output.
bulldozer_distance: Professional licensure, malpractice underwriting, state-by-state credentialing are structurally off-limits to labs/hyperscalers. 24-36mo.
pays_today_evidence: Telehealth already pays async clinician sign-off networks; AI vertical vendors repeatedly hit the 'needs a licensed human, who is liable' wall.
fast_cash_8wk: Yes: one AI healthtech/legaltech vendor routes managed sign-off on live volume within 8 weeks (panel contracted fast).
rough_25k_path: One vendor pays per-attestation pricing on live volume + setup.

name: Clearweight
one_liner: Model-lineage engine that says whether a specific open weight (and its full training-data + teacher-output ancestry) is legally safe to embed in a commercial product, with an indemnification-grade report.
buyer: GC / open-source-program-office / compliance at companies shipping on open weights + M&A diligence teams valuing AI startups.
wedge: Build the lineage graph (base -> fine-tunes -> teacher outputs -> licenses/ToS) and propagate the taint rule (a model distilled from GPT/Claude outputs is ToS-tainted even if redistributed 'open'). Start as per-model clearance reports via AI-focused law firms.
differentiation: Snyk/Black Duck do code SBOM; AIBOM lists a license string. Nobody propagates ToS-taint through model+training-data lineage to a defensible verdict. 10x = an indemnifiable legal opinion vs a license lookup.
bulldozer_distance: Hyperscalers ship AIBOM metadata not adversarial taint analysis/legal opinions; labs are conflicted (their ToS is the taint). Regulation locks demand. 24mo+.
pays_today_evidence: California AB 2013 (training-data summaries, eff Jan 1 2026); EU AI Act Aug 2 2026; OpenAI v DeepSeek + Anthropic distillation accusations make output-provenance litigable; messy licenses (Llama, 'Built with Qwen').
fast_cash_8wk: Yes: fixed-fee clearance reports ($5-20k) for the models an acquirer/GC needs cleared; partner with one AI law firm.
rough_25k_path: 3-6 clearance reports or one diligence engagement; convert to a 'watchlist' subscription.

name: Tendon
one_liner: Teleoperation + sim-to-real data flywheel producing proprietary dexterous-manipulation interaction data — Scale AI for robot hands.
buyer: Capital-rich, data-starved embodied-AI labs (Figure ~$39B, Skild $14B, Physical Intelligence, 1X).
wedge: Stand up a global teleop operator network + standardized rigs for one task family (contact-rich assembly/deformable manipulation); sell exclusive demo datasets + on-demand collection contracts.
differentiation: Real-world contact-rich trajectory data + sim domain-randomization that compounds into a dataset no lab can buy elsewhere; 10x vs Scale's static labeling which never captures manipulation trajectories.
bulldozer_distance: Data accrues only via operated fleets/rigs (Moravec); labs lack teleop ops + hardware-in-loop collection, slow to replicate. 24-36mo (risk: customer vertically integrates collection).
pays_today_evidence: $48.2B physical-AI funding 2026; whitespace names interaction data + transfer learning as THE bottleneck; labs pre-revenue but capital-rich and already buy data.
fast_cash_8wk: Medium: one paid collection contract / curated dataset sale to a funded robotics lab; teleop ops spin up fast.

name: Crucible
one_liner: Factory + marketplace of executable, verifiable task environments (gyms with reward functions) for training/evaluating agents — the new data layer for agent RL.
buyer: Frontier labs and well-funded agent startups doing RL/post-training (the Scale buyers, but for environments not labels).
wedge: Build 50-100 high-fidelity verifiable environments in one domain (realistic browser/ops/coding tasks with deterministic graders); sell as exclusive environment packs + custom-build contracts.
differentiation: Executable graded environments WITH verifiers (reproducible reward signals) not static labels; almost no one ships verifiable environments at scale, exactly what RL post-training needs.
bulldozer_distance: Labs are the BUYER not competitor (they outsource environment authoring); authoring+verifier expertise + compounding library is the moat. ~18mo (risk: labs in-house top packs).
pays_today_evidence: 2026 shift to executable RL environments as the binding input (Prime Intellect/Mechanize-style); labs starved for graded environments; data-centric AI underfunded vs model-centric.
fast_cash_8wk: Fast: labs/agent cos actively buying environments now; one contract closes in weeks.
